addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- code-simplifier:code-simplifier: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise. (Tools: All tools)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:code-simplifier: Use this agent when code has been written or modified and needs to be simplified for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. This agent should be triggered automatically after completing a coding task or writing a logical chunk of code. It simplifies code by following project best practices while retaining all functionality. The agent focuses only on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

Examples:

<example>
Context: The assistant has just implemented a new feature that adds user authentication to an API endpoint.
user: "Please add authentication to the /api/users endpoint"
assistant: "I've implemented the authentication for the /api/users endpoint. Here's the code:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
Since a logical chunk of code has been written (authentication feature), use the Task tool to launch the code-simplifier agent to improve the code's clarity and maintainability while preserving functionality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now let me use the code-simplifier agent to refine this implementation for better clarity and maintainability"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just fixed a bug by adding several conditional checks.
user: "Fix the null pointer exception in the data processor"
assistant: "I've added the necessary null checks to prevent the exception:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After modifying code to fix a bug, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the fix follows best practices and maintains code quality.
</commentary>
assistant: "Let me refine this bug fix using the code-simplifier agent to ensure it follows our best practices"
</example>

<example>
Context: The assistant has just refactored a function to improve performance.
user: "Optimize the data sorting algorithm for better performance"
assistant: "I've optimized the sorting algorithm. Here's the updated implementation:"
<function call omitted for brevity>
<commentary>
After completing a performance optimization task, use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and maintainable.
</commentary>
assistant: "Now I'll use the code-simplifier agent to ensure the optimized code is also clear and follows our coding standards"
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. This includes (1) after generating large documentation comments or docstrings, (2) before finalizing a pull request that adds or modifies comments, (3) when reviewing existing comments for potential technical debt or comment rot, and (4) when you need to verify that comments accurately reflect the code they describe. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:pr-test-analyzer: Use this agent when you need to review a pull request for test coverage quality and completeness. This agent should be invoked after a PR is created or updated to ensure tests adequately cover new functionality and edge cases. Typical triggers include the user asking whether tests on a freshly-created PR are thorough, an updated PR adding new logic that needs coverage analysis, and a final pre-merge double-check before marking a PR ready. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:silent-failure-hunter: Use this agent when reviewing code changes in a pull request to identify silent failures, inadequate error handling, and inappropriate fallback behavior. This agent should be invoked proactively after completing a logical chunk of work that involves error handling, catch blocks, fallback logic, or any code that could potentially suppress errors. Examples:

<example>
Context: Daisy has just finished implementing a new feature that fetches data from an API with fallback behavior.
Daisy: "I've added error handling to the API client. Can you review it?"
Assistant: "Let me use the silent-failure-hunter agent to thoroughly examine the error handling in your changes."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has created a PR with changes that include try-catch blocks.
Daisy: "Please review PR #1234"
Assistant: "I'll use the silent-failure-hunter agent to check for any silent failures or inadequate error handling in this PR."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example>

<example>
Context: Daisy has just refactored error handling code.
Daisy: "I've updated the error handling in the authentication module"
Assistant: "Let me proactively use the silent-failure-hunter agent to ensure the error handling changes don't introduce silent failures."
<Task tool invocation to launch silent-failure-hunter agent>
</example> (Tools: All tools)

- pr-review-toolkit:type-design-analyzer: Use this agent when you need expert analysis of type design in your codebase. Specifically use it (1) when introducing a new type to ensure it follows best practices for encapsulation and invariant expression, (2) during pull request creation to review all types being added, and (3) when refactoring existing types to improve their design quality. The agent will provide both qualitative feedback and quantitative ratings on encapsulation, invariant expression, usefulness, and enforcement. See "When to invoke" in the agent body for worked scenarios. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2

4	> Local working checklist for Week 7. Source of truth for task ticks stays
5	> [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md); this is the day-to-day scratch tracker.
6	>

9	---

16	  deferred live warehouse/file smoke).

18	  must be **live in Azure before any of Tracks B/C/D/E start**. The deploy still has two internal
19	  phases: **Phase 1 = deploy-ready hardening (prep)** → **Phase 2 = cloud apply + webhook + live
20	  smoke**.
21	- **Cloud apply is 🔒 blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner)** — this is the **gate for the
22	  whole week**. ⚠️ Strict cloud-first means everything downstream **pauses** until the Owner blocker
23	  clears; if RP registration slips, B/C/D/E don't proceed. (Resolving it is **Step 0** below.)

# DataQ — Week 7 working tracker (Deployment, hardening & docs)

> **NOT git-tracked** (matched by `.gitignore` → `*_TODO.md`, same as `HARNESS_TODO.md`).
> Local working checklist for Week 7. Source of truth for task ticks stays
> [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md); this is the day-to-day scratch tracker.
>
> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[-]` deferred/skipped · 🔒 blocked

---

## Context / sequencing

- **Harness is live end-to-end** (`HARNESS_TODO.md`, 2026-06-27): ADF + Airflow load
  Snowflake + UC + flat files; mockdata cron Jobs seeded. Only gate left on the harness =
  **DataQ's own cloud deploy** (its poller/check-runs read that data → discharges the
  deferred live warehouse/file smoke).
- **STRICT CLOUD-FIRST (user decision):** Track A (Deployment) is done **in full first** — DataQ
  must be **live in Azure before any of Tracks B/C/D/E start**. The deploy still has two internal
  phases: **Phase 1 = deploy-ready hardening (prep)** → **Phase 2 = cloud apply + webhook + live
  smoke**.
- **Cloud apply is 🔒 blocked on Azure RP registration (needs Owner)** — this is the **gate for the
  whole week**. ⚠️ Strict cloud-first means everything downstream **pauses** until the Owner blocker
  clears; if RP registration slips, B/C/D/E don't proceed. (Resolving it is **Step 0** below.)
- Harness issue-filing **out of scope**.

### What #379 already shipped — DON'T rebuild
- [x] `backend/Dockerfile` **and** `frontend/Dockerfile` + `frontend/nginx.conf`
- [x] `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (`workflow_dispatch`-only; `backend` + `frontend` jobs)
- [x] `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example`
- [x] `backend/app/core/secrets.py::AzureKeyVaultStore` (coded, lazy-import, **0% cov**)
- [x] `backend/app/core/logging.py:104` opencensus `AzureLogHandler` (conn-string-gated)
- [x] CORS middleware in `main.py`; `settings.environment: Literal["dev","staging","prod"]`

---

## Track A (LEAD) — Deployment

### Phase 1 — deploy-ready hardening (buildable now). One PR each.
- [~] **A0 — container registry → GHCR (decided) + ADR 0023**
  - [x] write `docs/adr/0023-container-image-registry-ghcr.md` · index in `docs/adr/README.md` + CLAUDE.md §9
  - [ ] rewire `deploy.yml`: `ghcr.io` login via `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`packages: write`) instead of `az acr login`;
        push `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ github.sha }}`; drop `ACR_*` vars (combine w/ A1 SHA pin)
  - [ ] rewire `deploy/README.md` + `deploy/.env.app.prod.example`: GHCR topology; publish package **public**
        → ACA pulls anonymously, **no stored registry credential** (GH PAT only if ever made private)
  - [ ] **overlaps A1** (both touch `deploy.yml`) — land as one PR, or A0 first + A1 rebased
- [ ] **A1 — CI/CD safety gate (`deploy.yml`)** · closes [#382](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/382)
  - [ ] `frontend` job gets `needs: backend` (no partial deploy vs un-rolled API)
  - [ ] pin `IMAGE` to `${{ github.sha }}` (not mutable `latest`) — migrate/api/worker same build
  - [ ] keep `workflow_dispatch`-only (do **not** uncomment `push:[main]` yet)
- [ ] **A2 — prod-docs gate** · closes [#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170)
  - [ ] `main.py:55` → `docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url = None` when `environment == "prod"`
  - [ ] test: prod → all None; dev → present
- [ ] **A3 — App Insights integration** (no GH issue; roadmap task)
  - [ ] extend the conn-string-gated seam in `core/logging.py` (no 2nd config path)
  - [ ] prefer `azure-monitor-opentelemetry` / OTLP (ADR 0010 vendor-neutral); **no-op when
        conn string empty** (local/CI unaffected)
  - [ ] instrument FastAPI request spans + Celery task spans (propagate `request_id`) +
        slow-query hook
  - [ ] pin dep in `backend/requirements.txt`; `pip-audit` clean; mocked enabled-vs-no-op test
- [ ] **A4 — real-vault integration test for `AzureKeyVaultStore`** (buildable half of #169; [#56 nit])
  - [ ] mock `DefaultAzureCredential` + `SecretClient`; assert `get`/`set` lazy-import branch (cov > 0)
- [ ] **A5 — silent token refresh** · closes [#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168)
  - [ ] axios interceptor: `InteractionRequiredAuthError` → interactive redirect fallback
  - [ ] vitest for silent-fail → interactive branch (real-AAD smoke is Phase 2)
- [ ] **A6 — verify frontend container + flip task**
  - [ ] build `frontend/Dockerfile`; nginx serves `dist` + proxies `/api/*`
  - [ ] flip "Containerise…" ⬜→✅ in `docs/progress.md`

### Phase 2 — cloud apply 🔒 (gated on Owner / Azure RP registration) — runbook, not PRs
- [ ] Provision ACR + ACA env + dataq-api/worker/migrate + SWA; set GitHub secrets/vars
      (`deploy/README.md`); add OIDC federated credential
- [ ] **Real Key Vault** [#169](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/169) — provision vault,
      grant api/worker MI *Key Vault Secrets User*, `SECRET_STORE=azure_key_vault`
- [ ] Run **Deploy** (`workflow_dispatch`) green E2E → optionally uncomment `push:[main]` (safe post-A1)
- [ ] **Azure Monitor → ADF webhook** (5 roadmap tasks): Action Group + Alert Rule (pre-prod + prod
      factories) → deliberate-failure smoke (<5 min); relates [#92](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/92)
- [ ] **Discharge deferred live smoke** vs harness Flows A/B/C → flip ⬜ in progress.md + CLAUDE.md §13;
      A5 real-AAD smoke closes here

---

## Track B — FastMCP server (12 tasks, epic #176) — deploy-independent, `backend/app/mcp/`

- [ ] **B4** — LLM-optimised docstrings (LLM-facing, §10) + Claude Desktop E2E on the 4 canonical NL
      queries
- [ ] **B5** — README MCP connection config (Claude Desktop/Claude.ai/Copilot/Cursor); closes
      [#17](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/17)

## Track C — Consistency & transaction hardening — deploy-independent
- [ ] **C1** — trigger-dedup race [#308](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/308): partial
      unique index `runs(suite_id, triggered_by)` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (bwd-compat migration,
      up/down tested) — reachable now the harness polls concurrently
- [ ] **C2** — stuck-run reaper [#309](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/309): beat janitor
      fail/re-dispatch runs orphaned `queued`/`running` past threshold
- [ ] **C3** — minor robustness (no issue): concurrent check-edit → 409 not 500; explicit `rollback` in
      `get_db` except; document read-modify-write convention in CONTRIBUTING

## Track D — Hardening & docs
- [ ] **D1** — error-handling audit: consistent envelope across all endpoints (`core/errors.py`)
- [ ] **D2** — endpoint metadata sweep: `summary`/`description`/`tags`/`response_model` everywhere (pairs A2)
- [ ] **D3** — README + deploy guide + env-var reference; modular user docs
      [#288](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/288)
- [ ] **D4** — team onboarding session + feedback (process, not code)

## Track E — Prototype final polish (2 left, epic #176)

      omit "anomalies detected" + "avg. time to resolution"
- [ ] **E2** — visual-fidelity: self-hosted Inter + JetBrains Mono, motion/focus tokens, hover elevation,
      empty/loading states; keep semantic tokens (free post-v1 dark mode)

---

## Suggested order — STRICT CLOUD-FIRST (Track A fully live before B/C/D/E begin)
0. 🔒 **Unblock — resolve Azure RP registration (Owner).** The entire week is gated on this; nothing
   downstream starts until DataQ is deployed.

1	# DataQ — Live test/demo-data environment · local tracker
2

4	> external harness described in **ADR 0021** (`docs/adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md`).

6	> the DataQ repo — keep paths/links here pointing at wherever you stand that up.
7	>

9	> repeatable retail data environment exercising all three DataQ run paths + the ADF/Airflow trigger path.

16	> **STATUS 2026-06-28 — HARNESS IS LIVE END-TO-END.** All loads run on a clean slate:
17	> ADF event→`ORDERS_HEADER`, ADF timer→`CUSTOMERS`, Airflow file-sensor→`PAYMENTS`,
18	> Airflow CeleryExecutor schedule→9 Snowflake feeds + 8 UC reference dims, Airflow→Flow B
19	> medallion (UC raw/silver/gold). Snowflake `RETAIL` = 12 tables; UC `dataq_retail` =
20	> reference/raw/silver/gold. Orchestrators are **decoupled from DataQ** (pure ETL; DataQ
21	> discovers runs by polling). **Architecture + operational runbook + the full chain of
22	> hard-won infra gotchas are documented in `README.md` at the harness root — read it.**
23	> Remaining: DataQ check-runs against this data (gated on deploying DataQ to cloud) +
24	> Databricks read-only service principal (Snowflake LOADER/READER split is implemented).
25	
26	---
27	
28	## 0. Prerequisites (accounts + local tooling)
29	
30	- [x] Azure account/subscription enabled; resource group `dataq-rg` created in West US 2 (sub: `Azure subscription 1`)
31	- [x] Snowflake account enabled — `PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383` (AWS us-west-2, Standard); `snow connection test` OK; creds in `secrets.sh` + `~/.snowflake/connections.toml` (chmod 600)
32	- [x] Databricks workspace enabled — express setup (AWS us-east-2); Unity Catalog metastore `metastore_aws_us_east_2` auto-provisioned; PAT `dapi...` in `secrets.sh`; host `https://dbc-b16e0901-d85c.cloud.databricks.com`; workspace admin confirmed via SCIM API
33	- [x] Local tooling: `terraform` 1.15.7, `az` CLI 2.87.0, `snowflake-cli` (`snow` 3.21.0), Docker — all installed

38	## 1. Terraform — cloud infra bootstrap (external, not git-tracked)
39	
40	> Provisions the **datasources** (ADLS / Snowflake / UC) **and the compute platform**:
41	> a shared **Azure Container Apps Environment** hosting (a) **Airflow** (decision 2026-06-26 —
42	> on Container Apps, *not* local Docker, *not* ADF Managed Airflow which Microsoft retired for
43	> new instances on 2026-01-01) and (b) the **mockdata file-creation Job** (§2a), plus a
44	> **container registry** for both images and a small **Postgres** for Airflow metadata.
45	> ADF is used in its managed cloud form (its own Schedule/Storage-Event triggers).
46	> **Cloud Airflow unlocks real Event-Grid push** for the file-landing feeds (a laptop behind
47	> NAT couldn't receive it) — so event triggers can be true push, not just poll-sensors.

52	- [x] **Unity Catalog**: `dataq_retail` catalog + raw/silver/gold/reference schemas + grants to PAT user — **APPLIED**. The catalog itself was **created in the Databricks UI with Default Storage** (the default-storage-only metastore rejects API/Terraform catalog creation — "Metastore storage root URL does not exist") then **`terraform import`**ed with `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [storage_root] }`; schemas+grants created by TF

Full harness applied: 45 resources in state, `terraform plan` → "No changes."** Path cleared 5 plan-invisible blockers (each only surfaced at apply against the live APIs): (1) **RP registration** — the Contributor SP *could* self-register all 7 RPs after all; (2) **Postgres offer-restriction** in westus2 → centralus; (3) **Postgres ghost-name 409** → `-cus` rename; (4) **ACR Tasks disabled** → local docker build+push; (5) **legacy ADF Snowflake connector** rejected ("version not supported") → rewrote to **SnowflakeV2** (`azurerm_data_factory_linked_custom_service` + `azurerm_data_factory_custom_dataset` + `SnowflakeV2Sink`). Outputs available via `terraform output`.

61	- [x] **No Azure resource names leak into the DataQ repo** (ADR 0010/0013) — names live only in the harness `terraform/` (outside the repo, gitignored)

65	## 2. Retail data model + mock-data generators (external harness)
66	
67	> Domains → entities to generate. Believable retail data so checks/profiling/severity/trends look real.

69	> **Status: built — `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/mockdata/` (Python package, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib, no Faker).** `python -m mockdata backfill --tier S --seed 42 --out ./out` (CLI is subcommand-based now: `backfill`/`init`/`tick`). Verified tier-S run: **24 datasets** (retail core + the full extended domain — settlements, purchase_orders/suppliers, order_fulfillment/fulfillment_nodes, categories, date_dim, exchange_rates, discounts, cancellations — **all now built, nothing deferred**), referential integrity holds, **18 deliberate issues** land exactly per the manifest (ruff+black clean). Emits to a local `out/` tree shaped like the landing zone; ADLS upload / warehouse load is §3.
70	
71	- [x] **Product** — sku_id, style_id, category, colour, material, size, unit_price, active, created_at (`products`)
72	- [x] **Inventory** — on_hand, planned, daily snapshot with `load_ts` (`inventory_snapshot`, Parquet per date)
73	- [x] **Location** — store, address, open/close hours (`locations`)
74	- [x] **Orders** — `orders_header` (order_number, channel, customer, status, subtotal, tax, order_total, ts) ↔ `order_lines` (sku, qty, unit_price, line_total). `subtotal = sum(line_total)` tie-out shaped clean; intra-dataset reconciliation target #1
75	- [x] **Sales lifecycle** — placed · shipped · audited events carrying `order_number` (`sales_events`, Flow B bronze; rolls up to gold revenue for the cross-platform reconcile)
76	- [x] **Payments / Finance** — `payments` (method, status, captured_amount) + `refunds` (amount, reason); `order_total ↔ payment_captured ↔ refund` testbed live. **Settlement ledger now modelled** (`settlements` — net-amount mismatch issue seeded)
77	- [x] **Return / Cancellation** — `returns` table (order, sku, reason, channel, qty) + `cancelled` order status **and** a standalone `cancellations` entity (cancellation-consistency issue seeded)
78	- [x] **Logistics** — shipment/tracking/carrier via `tracking_events` (stable `tracking_number` across ready→in_transit→delivered batches). **Fulfilling node now modelled** (`fulfillment_nodes` + `order_fulfillment` — node-orphan issue seeded)
79	- [x] **Channel / Marketplace** — online · store · marketplace as a first-class `channels` dimension + `channel` FK on orders/returns/feedback (null-channel + orphan issues seeded)
80	- [x] **Guest / Customer** — `customers` (name, address, **email, phone** PII for #226) + `feedback` survey (rating, comment, email). Chatbot folded into feedback
81	- [x] **Cross-cutting** — `promotions` (type/value/coupon-style) + per-order `tax`, plus `exchange_rates` + standalone `discounts` now modelled
82	- [x] _Optional (domain realism) — **all now built** (no longer deferred):_
83	  - [x] **Supplier / Vendor + Purchase Orders** — `suppliers` + `purchase_orders` (`supply` feed; supplier-orphan + qty_received>ordered issues seeded)
84	  - [x] **Fulfillment node / DC / warehouse** — `fulfillment_nodes` + `order_fulfillment` (join target beyond the store `Location`)
85	  - [x] **Catalog hierarchy** — `categories` above Product (department/brand attributes + `category_id` FK → `CAT-0000` orphan issue seeded)
86	  - [x] **Calendar / date dimension** — `date_dim` (season for trend slicing; completeness-gap issue seeded)
87	
88	**Route map — which dataset lands on which DataQ run path** (decided; per-flow detail in §3):
89	
90	| Dataset | Route | Platform · format / layer |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| Logistics **tracking events** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **CSV** · batch-resolved (latest) |
93	| **Inventory daily snapshot** | Flow C — flat-file in place | ADLS · **Parquet** · latest-snapshot |
94	| **Orders** (header+line) · **Payments/Finance** · **Returns/Cancellations** · **Customer** master · **Product/SKU** master | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Snowflake** (SQL-heavy: custom-SQL, line-grain, reconciliation) |
95	| **Location** · **Promotions/pricing/coupons** · **Channel/Marketplace** | Flow A — load to warehouse | **Unity Catalog** (plain load — proves the UC runner *without* medallion) |
96	| **Feedback/survey/chatbot** · **Sales → revenue marts** | Flow B — medallion | **Databricks** bronze → silver → gold (UC) |
97	
98	> **Discipline — one system-of-record per entity.** Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` revenue is a *derived* aggregate that must reconcile **back** to it (Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` — the ADR 0014 `comparison` demo). Never author the same entity authoritatively in two places, or the reconciliation / FK checks measure generation noise instead of real DQ — the same guard as the key-consistency rule above.
99	>
100	> **Reserved-kind homes:** medallion **gold** tables host freshness/volume/anomaly monitors (ADR 0012 — daily aggregates that should refresh); the flat-file feeds host freshness + batch-resolution; the Snowflake↔Databricks pair hosts the cross-platform `comparison`.
101	
102	- [x] Generator script (Python, pandas+pyarrow+stdlib — **no Faker**, stdlib `random`+pools for referential control) emitting CSV/Parquet/NDJSON, laid out per the route map. **Emits to local `out/`** (ADLS upload is §3; the folder tree is already landing-zone-shaped)
103	- [x] **Keep shared keys consistent across domains AND across batch/incremental loads** — shared key-pool strategy in `mockdata/generate.py` (masters generated first, transactional tables draw from the pools). **Verified:**
104	  - `order_number` identical across **Orders · Sales · Return · Logistics · Payments · Refunds**
105	  - `sku_id` identical across **Product · Inventory · Orders · Returns**
106	  - one `tracking_number` stable across `ready_for_shipment → in_transit → delivered` across the 3 batch files (verified: identical set across all batches, 639 shipments tier-S)
107	  - _Verified clean:_ subtotal==sum(line_total) 733/800 (the 67 = deliberate dup-line injection), orphans/nulls exactly per manifest — the deliberate issues are the **only** inconsistencies.
108	- [x] **Shape ≥1 dataset for each reserved monitor kind** (ADR 0012) — done in the generator (built post-v1, shaped now):
109	  - **Freshness · Volume · Anomaly** — `inventory_snapshot` (timestamped `load_ts`, N daily batches; one snapshot thinned to ~30% for volume/anomaly; latest `load_ts` pushed +26h for a freshness fail)
110	  - **Schema drift** — newest `inventory_snapshot` renames `on_hand → qty_on_hand`
111	  - **Comparison / reconciliation** — `sum(line_total)+tax == order_total` (intra) + `order_total ↔ payment.captured ↔ refund` (cross-table) + `order_total ↔ gold.daily_revenue` (cross-platform via `sales_events`)
112	- [x] **Seed deliberate DQ issues** — nulls · dupes · FK orphans (`SKU-000000`/`CUST-000000`) · stale freshness · volume drop · schema drift · reconciliation mismatch. All recorded in `out/_dq_issues_manifest.json` with affected keys (the QA/QE agent §4 asserts failures land *exactly* there)
113	- [x] Parameterise volume (`--tier S/M/L/XL`, perf §6) + `--seed`; `--no-issues` for clean data; run command + full docs in `mockdata/README.md`
114	
115	---
116	
117	## 2a. Recurring cadence — incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)
118	
119	> The §2 generator is **one-shot** (backfills N snapshots in a single run). Freshness/
120	> volume/anomaly monitors only mean something if data keeps *arriving* on a cadence.
121	> **Three clocks — do not conflate:** (1) **data-arrival** (source produces new data —
122	> NEW, this section); (2) **load+trigger** (ADF/Airflow picks up the file → triggers
123	> DataQ — §3); (3) **check-run** (DataQ's W5 `schedules` cron, or driven by clock 2).
124	>
125	> **Decisions (2026-06-26) — two layers, don't collapse them:**
126	> - **File creation (clock 1, source-system simulator) = Container Apps Job on a cron schedule.**
127	>   Runs the `mockdata` image (`tick → write files to ADLS landing zone`). Chosen over a
128	>   Function App (pandas/pyarrow is a heavy *batch*, not lightweight event glue — Functions
129	>   add packaging/cold-start friction) and over OS cron; reuses DataQ's Container Apps platform
130	>   + image, cron built in, pay-per-run. **This is *upstream* of the orchestrator** (source drops
131	>   files), which is cleaner/more realistic than the orchestrator also producing.
132	> - **Load + trigger (clock 2) = ADF/Airflow** load to Snowflake/UC → on-success trigger DataQ
133	>   (the path under test). **Deliberately mix two trigger modalities — not all event-driven:**
134	>     - **Event / file-landing** (ADF Storage-Event trigger / Airflow file-sensor or dataset DAG) —
135	>       fires when the Job drops a file. Use for high-frequency/latency-sensitive feeds (e.g. orders).
136	>     - **Schedule / time** (ADF Schedule or tumbling-window trigger / Airflow cron `schedule`) —
137	>       fires on a clock, processes whatever landed in the window. Use for daily/batch feeds
138	>       (e.g. inventory, feedback). **Set the load schedule to fire *after* the file-creation Job**
139	>       (e.g. Job drops inventory 01:00 → scheduled load 01:15) so there's data to pick up.
140	>     - _Why both:_ exercises both ADF/Airflow trigger types **and** DataQ's pipeline-run monitoring +
141	>       10-min polling / gap-recovery fallback (the poll only meaningfully bites on scheduled pipelines).
142	>   Flow C (flat-file in place) has no load step — the Job's file drop *is* the arrival; DataQ reads
143	>   in place (its own W5 schedule or arrival event).
144	>   Proposed split (adjustable): **orders → event**, **tracking → event**, **inventory → schedule**,
145	>   **feedback → schedule** — one event + one schedule per warehouse target (Snowflake & UC) for parity.
146	> - **Required by the Job runtime:** Container Apps Jobs are **ephemeral** → the generator's
147	>   state must move off local disk to **blob** (ADLS). `state.py` needs a pluggable backend
148	>   (local FS today; ADLS blob for the Job). See work items below.
149	
150	- [x] **Generator `tick` mode** — `python -m mockdata tick --feed {orders,tracking,inventory,feedback}` emits **one** batch-suffixed file per dataset (`<name>_b000123.<fmt>`, append-style), not the full backfill. `init` subcommand seeds state + emits the reference loads once. (`mockdata/increment.py` + `state.py`)
151	- [x] **Persisted state** (`state/state.json` + `state/masters/*.parquet`): master pools + climbing id counters (`order_number` never collides) + open-shipment registry (a `tracking_number` from an orders tick gets `ready→in_transit→delivered` on successive tracking ticks). **Verified:** 2 order ticks → ranges 1–40 / 41–80, 0 collisions; tracking# identical across the 3 lifecycle batches; cross-feed FK integrity holds; ruff+black clean
152	- [x] **Per-feed cadence map** (cron) — documented in `mockdata/README.md`: orders `0 0,6,12,18 * * *` · tracking `0 0,12 * * *` · inventory `0 1 * * *` · feedback/reference `0 1 */2 * *`; masters via `init` (rare). Anomaly flags `--volume-drop`/`--stale`/`--schema-drift` + `--issues` per tick
153	- [x] **`state.py` pluggable backend** — `StateBackend` seam: `LocalStateBackend` (default) + `AdlsStateBackend` (Gen2 blob via `DefaultAzureCredential`, azure libs lazy-imported). `make_backend()` picks by URI (`adls://<acct>/<fs>/<prefix>` else local path). **Verified:** local tick flow intact post-refactor; ruff+black clean. ADLS impl coded, runnable against real ADLS post-apply
154	- [x] **`Dockerfile`** (`python:3.13-slim` + pandas/pyarrow/azure; `ENTRYPOINT python -m mockdata`) + `.dockerignore` (keeps secrets.sh/terraform/out/state out of context). **Verified:** image builds; in-container `init`+`tick` write the route layout; **state survives across separate container runs** (mounted vol here; ADLS backend in the Job); `azure SDK OK` in image
155	- [x] **emit output backend → ADLS** — factored the byte-storage primitive into a shared `storage.py` `Store` seam (`LocalStore` + `AdlsStore` + `make_store`, used by **both** `state.py` and `emit.py` — one ADLS-auth impl, no dup). `emit.py` routes every write (csv/parquet/json via buffers + jsonl `append_text`) through the store; `--out adls://<acct>/<fs>[/<prefix>]` now writes the landing zone directly. **Verified:** local backfill+tick intact; ruff+black clean; in the image, `make_store` routing + lazy azure import + DataLake client construct, and a write reaches the Azure auth layer (fails only on missing identity — managed identity supplies it in the Job). **Job is now fully standalone** (no mount needed); only live ADLS write awaits apply
156	- [x] **Container Apps Job (cron)** per feed — **APPLIED + SEEDED**: 4 jobs (`mockdata-orders`/`-tracking`/`-inventory`/`-feedback`) provisioned on their crons, pulling `dataqacr3erlgd.azurecr.io/mockdata:latest`. **`init` run once (2026-06-27)** against `adls://dataqharness3erlgd/landing` (state at `…/landing/state`, tier S seed 42) — seeded masters + reference loads + `state.json`; a verification `tick --feed orders` emitted batch `b000001` (304 rows) cleanly, so the scheduled ticks (same command) will run. _Re-run `init` after a `terraform destroy`/recreate (state lives in the storage account)._
157	- [x] **ADF/Airflow load — APPLIED + RUNNING LIVE**: Airflow CeleryExecutor (schedule DAGs `flow_a_snowflake_load` 9 feeds / `flow_a_uc_reference` 8 dims / `flow_b_medallion`; event DAG `flow_a_payments_event`), ADF `pl_flow_a_orders` (blob-event) + `pl_flow_a_customers` (timer). **No trigger push** — decoupled (DataQ polls; see §3a). The only remaining gate is DataQ's *own* deploy so its poller can read the ADF/Airflow REST APIs (separate deploy track). NB: the ADF blob-event trigger needed the ADF MI granted on the storage account to *fire* — a role assignment the Contributor SP can't make (manual grant)
158	- [ ] Verify a few cadence ticks produce **monotonic** freshness/volume series in DataQ `/results` (the monitors trend, not just one green dot)
159	
160	---
161	
162	## 3. Reference flows (each exercises a distinct DataQ run path)
163	
164	### 3a. Flow A — load to warehouse  →  `SnowflakeCheckRunner` / `UnityCatalogCheckRunner`
165	
166	> **Datasets:** **Snowflake** ← Orders (header+line) · Payments/Finance · Returns/Cancellations · Customer master · Product/SKU master (transactional + financial core; custom-SQL + line-grain + `sum(line_total)=order_total` + `order_total↔payment↔refund` reconciliation). **Unity Catalog (plain load)** ← Location · Promotions/pricing/coupons · Channel/Marketplace (governed reference data — proves the UC runner *without* the medallion machinery).
167	
168	> **Harness Airflow lives in `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/airflow/`** (image `Dockerfile` + `requirements.txt` + `dags/`). Image = `apache/airflow:2.10.5` + key-based client libs (snowflake-connector, databricks-sql, azure-datalake) + DAGs, running **CeleryExecutor** (Redis broker + a scalable worker app — LocalExecutor fork-deadlocks on Container Apps; see README gotcha #7). **DECOUPLED FROM DATAQ: no callback — `dataq_airflow_callback.py` was deleted; orchestrators run pure ETL and DataQ discovers runs by polling the Airflow REST API (ADR 0007).** All loads = whole-subdir overwrite (idempotent; per-file watermark = follow-up).
169	
170	- [x] File lands in ADLS landing zone (from §2 generator) — via the §2a mockdata cron Jobs
171	- [x] **Orchestrator picks up + loads — RUNNING LIVE END-TO-END** — **Airflow** (schedule): `flow_a_snowflake_load` (**9 feeds** → Snowflake `write_pandas`) + `flow_a_uc_reference` (**8 reference dims** → UC, databricks-sql chunked insert) + `flow_b_medallion` (bronze→silver→gold); **Airflow** (event): `flow_a_payments_event` → Snowflake `PAYMENTS` — **now TRUE Event Grid push** (2026-06-28): ADLS `BlobCreated` on `snowflake/payments/*.csv` → Event Grid → `dataq-harness-airflow-trigger` receiver Container App (`eventgrid_receiver/`) → Airflow REST `dagRuns`; DAG is `schedule=None`, the file-sensor poll is gone. **ADF** (event): `pl_flow_a_orders` blob-event trigger → SnowflakeV2 Copy → `ORDERS_HEADER`; **ADF** (timer): `pl_flow_a_customers` schedule trigger → `CUSTOMERS`. All four modalities verified loading on a clean slate (Event-Grid push verified end-to-end: blob overwrite → ~2s → triggered run `success`)
172	- [-] ~~On success, the orchestrator hits DataQ's trigger path~~ — **REMOVED (decoupling, 2026-06-27).** No push from the orchestrator: no Airflow callback, no ADF Web-activity webhook. DataQ (one of several possible monitors) discovers runs by **polling** the ADF/Airflow REST APIs (ADR 0006/0007) — the orchestrators stay pure ETL
173	- [~] DataQ suite runs against the loaded **warehouse table**; result row persists — suites + trigger-bindings specced in `dataq_setup/` (`flow_a_snowflake`, `flow_a_uc_reference`); live run apply+deploy-gated
174	- [ ] Verify in `/results`: run succeeded, severity derived, metric_value populated — apply+deploy-gated
175	- [~] **Snowflake suite** exercises a **custom-SQL** check (ADR 0019) — `order_total <> ROUND(SUM(line_total)*1.08,2)` reconciliation specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`; create at deploy
176	- [~] **UC plain-load suite** on Location/Promotions — `flow_a_uc_reference` suite specced in `dataq_setup/` (no Silver/Gold — that's Flow B)
177	
178	### 3b. Flow B — medallion  →  UC tables
179	
180	> **Datasets:** **Feedback/survey/chatbot** (bronze raw JSON/text → silver cleaned + **PII-handled** [#226] → gold sentiment/NPS feature table — the lakehouse/ML "feature store" story) and **Sales → revenue marts** (bronze raw sales events → silver cleaned daily sales by store·channel → gold revenue/units rollups).
181	
182	- [x] File lands in bronze — generator drops `databricks_medallion/bronze/{sales_events,feedback}/*.json`; notebook reads them into UC `raw`
183	- [x] **Databricks notebook** runs bronze → silver → gold — `databricks/medallion_notebook.py` (key-based ADLS via spark conf; raw load → silver clean+PII-hash → gold daily_revenue rollup). Submitted by `flow_b_medallion` DAG via the Databricks SDK (parses clean in the image)
184	- [~] DataQ suites on UC Silver/Gold — `flow_b_silver`/`flow_b_gold` specced in `dataq_setup/`; live apply+deploy-gated
185	- [x] **Gold hosts the reserved freshness/volume/anomaly targets** — `gold.daily_revenue` (daily rollup) is the shaped target
186	- [x] **Cross-platform reconciliation target** (ADR 0014) — Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` (sales_events `placed.amount` = order_total → rolls up to tie out); the `comparison` demo shaped
187	- [x] notebook completion triggers DataQ — `flow_b_medallion` DAG `on_dataq_success` → `(airflow, flow_b_medallion)` binding
188	- [ ] Verify results per layer (bronze schema-drift, silver PII-redaction, gold freshness/volume) — apply+deploy-gated
189	
190	### 3c. Flow C — flat-file in place  →  `FlatFileCheckRunner` + batch resolution
191	
192	> **Datasets:** **Logistics tracking events** (ADLS · **CSV** · event-append — `tracking_number` stable across `Ready For Shipment → In Transit → Delivered` batches) and **Inventory daily snapshot** (ADLS · **Parquet** · daily-snapshot — exercises the zero-copy Arrow read, PR #140). One of each format × each batch shape (event-append vs daily-snapshot).
193	
194	- [x] File lands in ADLS and **stays a flat file** (no warehouse load) — `adls_flatfile/{logistics_tracking,inventory_snapshot}/`, no orchestrator load step
195	- [x] DataQ flat-file connection + suite targets the path with a **batching regex** — specced in `dataq_setup/README.md`: tracking `tracking_events_(.+)\.csv`, inventory `inventory_snapshot_(.+)\.parquet`, strategy `latest` (first capture group = batch key). No trigger-binding — runs on DataQ's own schedule
196	- [ ] Run resolves the **latest** batch and checks it directly — apply+deploy-gated
197	- [ ] Verify batch resolution picks the right file + result persists — apply+deploy-gated
198	- [~] **CSV** path: tracking events — checks specced (tracking-number/event-status + freshness) in `dataq_setup/`
199	- [~] **Parquet** path: inventory snapshot — checks specced (volume band ±20% + freshness on latest) in `dataq_setup/`
200	
201	---
202	
203	## 4. QA/QE agent — module-by-module + E2E (this IS git-tracked: `.claude/agents/`)
204	
205	> The one in-repo deliverable. A Claude Code subagent, sibling to `migration-safety` /
206	> `orchestration-abstraction-guard`. Lands in Week 8 / ongoing.
207	
208	- [ ] Define agent scope: per-module functional checks (connections, suites, checks, run paths, profiler, results, orchestration) + full E2E against the harness
209	- [ ] Write `.claude/agents/qa-qe.md` (tools, model, system prompt)
210	- [ ] Wire it to drive the three flows above as E2E smoke once the harness is up
211	- [ ] Decide invocation cadence (per-PR? on-demand? pre-release?)
212	- [ ] **This file is committed** — open a PR per working agreements (branch `feature/qa-qe-agent`)
213	
214	---
215	
216	## 5. HashiCorp Vault — secret-management spike (post-v1 / backlog)
217	
218	> Validates the `SecretStore` seam (ADR 0010/0013): Azure Key Vault is **one** impl, not the architecture.
219	
220	- [ ] Stand up Vault locally (dev mode / Docker) in the harness
221	- [ ] Prototype a `VaultSecretStore` against the existing `SecretStore` interface (spike branch, not merged)
222	- [ ] Confirm no interface change needed (if it needs one, that's a finding worth an ADR note)
223	- [ ] Write up: does the seam hold? effort to make Vault a first-class option? → backlog issue / ADR follow-up
224	
225	---
226	
227	## 6. Performance / scale testing (post-v1 / backlog)
228	
229	> Rides §2's parameterizable volume (`--seed` + a `--volume` knob): generate **S / M / L / XL** of the same
230	> datasets, run, measure, record. **Baseline-first** — establish numbers + a regression budget; no hard SLAs
231	> yet (set them once we know where the knees are). Standalone harness script; the **QA/QE agent (§4)** can
232	> invoke it later as a perf-smoke. Needs §2 (generators) + §3 (the run paths) green first.
233	
234	**Lead axes (do these first):**
235	- [ ] **Run scaling** *(headline)* — GX run wall-clock vs **rows × #checks-per-suite**, per datasource (Snowflake · UC · flat-file CSV+Parquet). Capture throughput (rows/sec) + **worker memory peak** (GX pulls the batch in-memory) — and note whether time is in the warehouse query or GX itself.
236	- [ ] **Profiler latency** — profile a **wide** table (many columns) to quantify the known **N+1 per-column top-values** cost ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)) and the payoff once it's batched.
237	- [ ] **DB growth** — p95 latency of `/results`, `/dashboard/summary`, `/runs` as `results`/`runs` grow to 100k+ rows; validates the retention-sweep index ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)).
238	
239	**Secondary axes:**
240	- [ ] **Concurrency / throughput** — N suites at once: Celery pool saturation, the 60s scheduler beat under many due schedules (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), run-now storms → queue wait time.
241	- [ ] **Flat-file batch resolution at scale** — resolving the latest batch among a large landing zone (thousands of files) — the list+regex cost.
242	
243	**Method:**
244	- [ ] One script: param = datasource × volume tier × #checks; emits a CSV/JSON of (metric, value) per run
245	- [ ] Record a **baseline table** (commit it with the QA/QE agent in §4) + a **regression budget** (e.g. fail if a tier regresses > X% vs baseline) — so perf regressions are caught, not just measured once
246	- [ ] Capture both **app** metrics (run duration, profiler latency, API p95) and **resource** metrics (worker mem peak, DB CPU)
247	- [ ] Feed findings back: confirm/close #327 (profiler) + #323 (retention index); file new issues for any knee found
248	
249	---
250	
251	## Tracking notes
252	
253	- [ ] File the milestoned GitHub issues (per convention): Terraform → W7 · mock-data + Flows A/B/C → W7 · QA/QE agent → W8 · Vault → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)" · perf/scale testing → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)"  *(currently NOT filed — pending decision)*
254	- [ ] When a flow first runs green end-to-end, update `docs/progress.md` (flip the ⬜ in the workstream table) and note the live smoke is discharged in CLAUDE.md §13

# DataQ — Live test/demo-data environment · local tracker

> **NOT git-tracked** (matched by `.gitignore` → `*_TODO.md`). This is the working tracker for the
> external harness described in **ADR 0021** (`docs/adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md`).
> The harness code itself (Terraform, mock-data generators, Databricks notebook) also lives **outside**
> the DataQ repo — keep paths/links here pointing at wherever you stand that up.
>
> Purpose: discharge the standing *"deferred live warehouse/file run smoke"* caveat with a real,
> repeatable retail data environment exercising all three DataQ run paths + the ADF/Airflow trigger path.
>
> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[-]` skipped/deferred

Suggested order: **0 → 1 → 2 → (3a/3b/3c in parallel) → 4 → 5 → 6** (perf rides §2 volume + §3 run paths).
Harness root: `~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/` · secrets: `secrets.sh` (chmod 600, never committed)

> **STATUS 2026-06-28 — HARNESS IS LIVE END-TO-END.** All loads run on a clean slate:
> ADF event→`ORDERS_HEADER`, ADF timer→`CUSTOMERS`, Airflow file-sensor→`PAYMENTS`,
> Airflow CeleryExecutor schedule→9 Snowflake feeds + 8 UC reference dims, Airflow→Flow B
> medallion (UC raw/silver/gold). Snowflake `RETAIL` = 12 tables; UC `dataq_retail` =
> reference/raw/silver/gold. Orchestrators are **decoupled from DataQ** (pure ETL; DataQ
> discovers runs by polling). **Architecture + operational runbook + the full chain of
> hard-won infra gotchas are documented in `README.md` at the harness root — read it.**
> Remaining: DataQ check-runs against this data (gated on deploying DataQ to cloud) +
> Databricks read-only service principal (Snowflake LOADER/READER split is implemented).

|---|---|---|

> **Discipline — one system-of-record per entity.** Snowflake `Orders` is the truth; the medallion `gold` revenue is a *derived* aggregate that must reconcile **back** to it (Snowflake `order_total` ↔ Databricks `gold.daily_revenue` — the ADR 0014 `comparison` demo). Never author the same entity authoritatively in two places, or the reconciliation / FK checks measure generation noise instead of real DQ — the same guard as the key-consistency rule above.
>
> **Reserved-kind homes:** medallion **gold** tables host freshness/volume/anomaly monitors (ADR 0012 — daily aggregates that should refresh); the flat-file feeds host freshness + batch-resolution; the Snowflake↔Databricks pair hosts the cross-platform `comparison`.

## 2a. Recurring cadence — incremental loads (the "source keeps producing" clock)

> The §2 generator is **one-shot** (backfills N snapshots in a single run). Freshness/
> volume/anomaly monitors only mean something if data keeps *arriving* on a cadence.
> **Three clocks — do not conflate:** (1) **data-arrival** (source produces new data —
> NEW, this section); (2) **load+trigger** (ADF/Airflow picks up the file → triggers
> DataQ — §3); (3) **check-run** (DataQ's W5 `schedules` cron, or driven by clock 2).
>
> **Decisions (2026-06-26) — two layers, don't collapse them:**
> - **File creation (clock 1, source-system simulator) = Container Apps Job on a cron schedule.**
>   Runs the `mockdata` image (`tick → write files to ADLS landing zone`). Chosen over a
>   Function App (pandas/pyarrow is a heavy *batch*, not lightweight event glue — Functions
>   add packaging/cold-start friction) and over OS cron; reuses DataQ's Container Apps platform
>   + image, cron built in, pay-per-run. **This is *upstream* of the orchestrator** (source drops
>   files), which is cleaner/more realistic than the orchestrator also producing.
> - **Load + trigger (clock 2) = ADF/Airflow** load to Snowflake/UC → on-success trigger DataQ
>   (the path under test). **Deliberately mix two trigger modalities — not all event-driven:**
>     - **Event / file-landing** (ADF Storage-Event trigger / Airflow file-sensor or dataset DAG) —
>       fires when the Job drops a file. Use for high-frequency/latency-sensitive feeds (e.g. orders).
>     - **Schedule / time** (ADF Schedule or tumbling-window trigger / Airflow cron `schedule`) —
>       fires on a clock, processes whatever landed in the window. Use for daily/batch feeds
>       (e.g. inventory, feedback). **Set the load schedule to fire *after* the file-creation Job**
>       (e.g. Job drops inventory 01:00 → scheduled load 01:15) so there's data to pick up.
>     - _Why both:_ exercises both ADF/Airflow trigger types **and** DataQ's pipeline-run monitoring +
>       10-min polling / gap-recovery fallback (the poll only meaningfully bites on scheduled pipelines).
>   Flow C (flat-file in place) has no load step — the Job's file drop *is* the arrival; DataQ reads
>   in place (its own W5 schedule or arrival event).
>   Proposed split (adjustable): **orders → event**, **tracking → event**, **inventory → schedule**,
>   **feedback → schedule** — one event + one schedule per warehouse target (Snowflake & UC) for parity.
> - **Required by the Job runtime:** Container Apps Jobs are **ephemeral** → the generator's
>   state must move off local disk to **blob** (ADLS). `state.py` needs a pluggable backend
>   (local FS today; ADLS blob for the Job). See work items below.

- [x] **Generator `tick` mode** — `python -m mockdata tick --feed {orders,tracking,inventory,feedback}` emits **one** batch-suffixed file per dataset (`<name>_b000123.<fmt>`, append-style), not the full backfill. `init` subcommand seeds state + emits the reference loads once. (`mockdata/increment.py` + `state.py`)
- [x] **Persisted state** (`state/state.json` + `state/masters/*.parquet`): master pools + climbing id counters (`order_number` never collides) + open-shipment registry (a `tracking_number` from an orders tick gets `ready→in_transit→delivered` on successive tracking ticks). **Verified:** 2 order ticks → ranges 1–40 / 41–80, 0 collisions; tracking# identical across the 3 lifecycle batches; cross-feed FK integrity holds; ruff+black clean
- [x] **Per-feed cadence map** (cron) — documented in `mockdata/README.md`: orders `0 0,6,12,18 * * *` · tracking `0 0,12 * * *` · inventory `0 1 * * *` · feedback/reference `0 1 */2 * *`; masters via `init` (rare). Anomaly flags `--volume-drop`/`--stale`/`--schema-drift` + `--issues` per tick
- [x] **`state.py` pluggable backend** — `StateBackend` seam: `LocalStateBackend` (default) + `AdlsStateBackend` (Gen2 blob via `DefaultAzureCredential`, azure libs lazy-imported). `make_backend()` picks by URI (`adls://<acct>/<fs>/<prefix>` else local path). **Verified:** local tick flow intact post-refactor; ruff+black clean. ADLS impl coded, runnable against real ADLS post-apply
- [x] **`Dockerfile`** (`python:3.13-slim` + pandas/pyarrow/azure; `ENTRYPOINT python -m mockdata`) + `.dockerignore` (keeps secrets.sh/terraform/out/state out of context). **Verified:** image builds; in-container `init`+`tick` write the route layout; **state survives across separate container runs** (mounted vol here; ADLS backend in the Job); `azure SDK OK` in image
- [x] **emit output backend → ADLS** — factored the byte-storage primitive into a shared `storage.py` `Store` seam (`LocalStore` + `AdlsStore` + `make_store`, used by **both** `state.py` and `emit.py` — one ADLS-auth impl, no dup). `emit.py` routes every write (csv/parquet/json via buffers + jsonl `append_text`) through the store; `--out adls://<acct>/<fs>[/<prefix>]` now writes the landing zone directly. **Verified:** local backfill+tick intact; ruff+black clean; in the image, `make_store` routing + lazy azure import + DataLake client construct, and a write reaches the Azure auth layer (fails only on missing identity — managed identity supplies it in the Job). **Job is now fully standalone** (no mount needed); only live ADLS write awaits apply
- [x] **Container Apps Job (cron)** per feed — **APPLIED + SEEDED**: 4 jobs (`mockdata-orders`/`-tracking`/`-inventory`/`-feedback`) provisioned on their crons, pulling `dataqacr3erlgd.azurecr.io/mockdata:latest`. **`init` run once (2026-06-27)** against `adls://dataqharness3erlgd/landing` (state at `…/landing/state`, tier S seed 42) — seeded masters + reference loads + `state.json`; a verification `tick --feed orders` emitted batch `b000001` (304 rows) cleanly, so the scheduled ticks (same command) will run. _Re-run `init` after a `terraform destroy`/recreate (state lives in the storage account)._

## 5. HashiCorp Vault — secret-management spike (post-v1 / backlog)

> Validates the `SecretStore` seam (ADR 0010/0013): Azure Key Vault is **one** impl, not the architecture.

- [ ] Stand up Vault locally (dev mode / Docker) in the harness
- [ ] Prototype a `VaultSecretStore` against the existing `SecretStore` interface (spike branch, not merged)
- [ ] Confirm no interface change needed (if it needs one, that's a finding worth an ADR note)
- [ ] Write up: does the seam hold? effort to make Vault a first-class option? → backlog issue / ADR follow-up

---

## 6. Performance / scale testing (post-v1 / backlog)

> Rides §2's parameterizable volume (`--seed` + a `--volume` knob): generate **S / M / L / XL** of the same
> datasets, run, measure, record. **Baseline-first** — establish numbers + a regression budget; no hard SLAs
> yet (set them once we know where the knees are). Standalone harness script; the **QA/QE agent (§4)** can
> invoke it later as a perf-smoke. Needs §2 (generators) + §3 (the run paths) green first.

**Lead axes (do these first):**
- [ ] **Run scaling** *(headline)* — GX run wall-clock vs **rows × #checks-per-suite**, per datasource (Snowflake · UC · flat-file CSV+Parquet). Capture throughput (rows/sec) + **worker memory peak** (GX pulls the batch in-memory) — and note whether time is in the warehouse query or GX itself.
- [ ] **Profiler latency** — profile a **wide** table (many columns) to quantify the known **N+1 per-column top-values** cost ([#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327)) and the payoff once it's batched.
- [ ] **DB growth** — p95 latency of `/results`, `/dashboard/summary`, `/runs` as `results`/`runs` grow to 100k+ rows; validates the retention-sweep index ([#323](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/323)).

**Secondary axes:**
- [ ] **Concurrency / throughput** — N suites at once: Celery pool saturation, the 60s scheduler beat under many due schedules (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), run-now storms → queue wait time.
- [ ] **Flat-file batch resolution at scale** — resolving the latest batch among a large landing zone (thousands of files) — the list+regex cost.

**Method:**
- [ ] One script: param = datasource × volume tier × #checks; emits a CSV/JSON of (metric, value) per run
- [ ] Record a **baseline table** (commit it with the QA/QE agent in §4) + a **regression budget** (e.g. fail if a tier regresses > X% vs baseline) — so perf regressions are caught, not just measured once
- [ ] Capture both **app** metrics (run duration, profiler latency, API p95) and **resource** metrics (worker mem peak, DB CPU)
- [ ] Feed findings back: confirm/close #327 (profiler) + #323 (retention index); file new issues for any knee found

---

## Tracking notes

- [ ] File the milestoned GitHub issues (per convention): Terraform → W7 · mock-data + Flows A/B/C → W7 · QA/QE agent → W8 · Vault → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)" · perf/scale testing → "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)"  *(currently NOT filed — pending decision)*
- [ ] When a flow first runs green end-to-end, update `docs/progress.md` (flip the ⬜ in the workstream table) and note the live smoke is discharged in CLAUDE.md §13

Extracted the shared GX machinery — snake_case→GX-class translation, GX-result→`SuiteOutcome` mapping, and the suite/validation-definition `run_expectations` flow — into `datasources/gx_runner.py` (reused by the Snowflake runner and the future UC runner; no duplication). Flat-file IO (`download_bytes` + full-file `read_dataframe`) moved to `flatfile.py` behind primitives (raw config + resolved secret, not the ORM — matches `build_snowflake_runner`), and the column profiler now shares it. Because GX runs **in-process on the DataFrame**, the run path is fully unit-tested with a canned frame (real GX execution, not mocked) — only the network download is the deferred-smoke seam. 16 new tests; `gx_runner` 100%, `flatfile` 76% (only `download_bytes` uncovered). _Connection-type → runner dispatch (Snowflake vs flat-file) is the Week-5 `run_suite` wiring; **freshness-by-filename** is the reserved `freshness` monitor kind (deferred, ADR 0012), not a GX expectation._

161:- [x] ✅ Check types for flat files: schema validation, row count, null checks, ~~freshness by filename date~~ — `FlatFileCheckRunner` (`datasources/flatfile.py`): downloads the file (S3/ADLS) into a pandas DataFrame and runs the suite's GX expectations against GX's **pandas DataFrame asset**, so all GX expectation-kind checks (schema/row-count/null/value checks) run against flat files. Extracted the shared GX machinery — snake_case→GX-class translation, GX-result→`SuiteOutcome` mapping, and the suite/validation-definition `run_expectations` flow — into `datasources/gx_runner.py` (reused by the Snowflake runner and the future UC runner; no duplication).
