############### #175 ###############
Tracking epic for **Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting`.

**COMPLETE — 27 / 27.** Exit gate met: full results dashboard across all source types + alerts firing with suppression. All Week-6 work landed as the stacked PR chain **#366→#379** (open, each green + reviewed); closeout docs #380.

**Results dashboard**
- [x] Health score stat cards + 7-day trend chart (PR #333)
- [x] Per-suite pass / fail progress bars — warn / fail / critical breakdown (PR #333)
- [x] Results filter bar — by env, datasource type, suite, date range, status
- [x] Failed check drill-down — sample failing rows from GX result _(redacted rows, PR #365, closes #226)_
- [x] Per-check historical trend chart (PR #333 — run-detail expandable row)
- [x] ADF pipeline status panel — polls every 30s; correlated DQ result
- [x] Datasource type filter — Snowflake / flat file / Unity Catalog
- [x] CSV + PDF export of results _(CSV + JSON shipped PR #333; **PDF deferred to backlog**)_
- [x] **Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results** (PR #333)
- [x] Severity badge colours — green/amber/red/dark red (PR-C1)
- [x] Health score weighting — warn/fail/critical penalty weights (PR #333, ADR 0005)

**Alerting**
- [x] \`ResultPublisher\` seam — dispatch run outcomes through a publisher interface (Teams = v1 impl); PII policy on \`sample_failures\` at the seam (ADR 0011) — **PR #366**
- [x] Notification config UI — Teams webhook per suite, alert on fail / warn / always — backend **PR #373** + UI **PR #378**
- [x] Alert suppression / snooze — silence a specific check for N hours — **PR #370**
- [x] Alert dedup — fire on first failure only, not on every subsequent scheduled run — **PR #369**
- [x] Teams adaptive card payload — check, datasource, table / file, observed vs expected — **PR #367**
- [x] Severity-aware alert routing — warn quiet, fail standard, critical @channel — **PR #368**

**Prototype adoption — full screen set as dedicated-page flows (ADR 0022)**
- [x] Nav / shell restructure (PR #333)
- [x] Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard page (`/dashboard`) — recharts (PR #333)
- [x] New Suite → dedicated page (Phase 4.1)
- [x] Edit flows → dedicated pages (Phase 4.2; dedups #204)
- [x] Results drill-down → dedicated route (`/results/:runId`) — #268 (PR #333)
- [x] Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx) — `ErrorState` + router catch-all + ErrorBoundary — **PR #377**
- [x] New Connection → source-select + config (2-page flow) (Phase 4.3)
- [x] Profile page — real content (`/profile`) — **PR #374**
- [x] Workspace Settings page (`/settings`) — **PR #375**
- [x] Admin page — reconcile layout to prototype (KPI cards + tables, no tabs) — **PR #376**

_Week-7 early-credit: Azure deploy scaffolding **PR #379**. Follow-ups filed: #371 (validation-handler ctx), #372 (SecretStore.delete cleanup)._
############### #177 ###############
Tracking epic for **Week 8 — Unit testing & coverage (≥80%)**. Mirrors the roadmap; the task-level source of truth remains [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md). Milestone: `Week 8 — Unit testing & coverage (≥80%)`.


**Backend unit tests (pytest)**
- [ ] Auth service — token validation, session expiry, Key Vault credential retrieval
- [ ] Connection service — CRUD operations, test endpoint logic per datasource type
- [ ] Suite service — CRUD, share assignment, export / import serialisation
- [ ] Check service — expectation builder (form path), SQL check validator, dry-run logic, threshold tier evaluation (warn/fail/critical status derivation from observed value + three thresholds, binary fallback when tiers not set)
- [ ] Column profiler service — null count, distinct count, min/max per datasource type
- [ ] Execution service — Celery task dispatch, progress polling, cancel logic, retention purge
- [ ] Alerting service — Teams webhook dispatch, dedup logic, snooze / suppression
- [ ] ADF service — webhook payload parsing (valid schema, missing fields, wrong secret → 401), follow-up REST API call for run details, upsert logic, gap recovery dedup
- [ ] ADF polling service — succeeded run fetch, skip-if-recently-updated logic, startup gap recovery
- [ ] Result service — health score calculation, historical trend aggregation, export generation
- [ ] MCP service — each of the 8 tools returns correct response shape; auth rejection on missing/invalid token; `trigger_suite_run` returns valid `run_id`; `create_check` persists to DB correctly

**API layer tests (pytest + httpx)**
- [ ] Auth endpoints — login redirect, token refresh, unauthorised access returns 401
- [ ] Connection endpoints — CRUD happy paths + validation errors
- [ ] Suite & check endpoints — CRUD, share, export / import, dry-run
- [ ] Execution endpoints — trigger run, poll progress, cancel, list history
- [ ] Results endpoints — dashboard data, drill-down, filters, download
- [ ] ADF webhook endpoint — valid payload returns 200, invalid secret returns 401, malformed payload returns 422, duplicate runId is idempotent

**Frontend unit tests (Vitest + React Testing Library)**
- [ ] Login screen — Azure AD button renders, redirects on click
- [ ] Connection manager — card renders per type, status badge colours, re-auth flow
- [ ] Check editor — form fields render per check type, column profiler loads, dry-run shows result, severity tier toggle shows / hides three threshold inputs correctly
- [ ] Suite sharing panel — add/remove user, role assignment
- [ ] Execution page — run now triggers correctly, progress bar updates, cancel button
- [ ] Results dashboard — stat cards render correct values, filters update table, ADF panel shows status, severity badges render correct colour for pass / warn / fail / critical

**Test infrastructure**
- [ ] Pytest fixtures — mock GX context, mock Snowflake / ADLS / UC connections, mock Celery, mock Azure Monitor webhook payload, mock ADF REST API run detail response
- [ ] CI gate — PRs blocked if unit test coverage drops below 80%
- [ ] Test data fixtures — sample suites, check results, run histories for consistent assertions


**QA/QE testing agent (added — not in original roadmap, ADR [0021](../blob/main/docs/adr/0021-demo-test-data-environment-strategy.md))**
> A Claude Code subagent (sibling to `migration-safety` / `orchestration-abstraction-guard`) that tests the DataQ framework module-by-module **and** E2E against the external demo-data harness. The one **in-repo** deliverable of the live test/demo-data environment workstream; detailed steps in the local `HARNESS_TODO.md`.
- [ ] Define agent scope — per-module functional checks (connections, suites, checks, run paths, profiler, results, orchestration) + full E2E
- [ ] Write `.claude/agents/qa-qe.md` (tools, model, system prompt)
- [ ] Wire it to drive the three reference flows (A/B/C) as E2E smoke once the harness is up
- [ ] Decide invocation cadence (per-PR / on-demand / pre-release)

      - uses: actions/checkout@v7
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      # Betterleaks has no official GitHub Action; run the official pinned
      # container image. Drop-in gitleaks replacement: nonzero exit on findings
      # fails the job. safe.directory env is required because the container runs
      # as root while checkout writes files as the runner user — without it git
      # aborts with "detected dubious ownership".
      - name: Run betterleaks
        run: |
          docker run --rm \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=safe.directory \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=/repo \
            -v "$PWD:/repo" -w /repo \
            ghcr.io/betterleaks/betterleaks:v1.4.1 \
            git /repo --config /repo/.betterleaks.toml --redact --verbose

  # ── Frontend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  frontend-lint:
    name: Frontend — lint & format

content.text: This is the classic full-history-scan problem. Two things jump out: `fetch-depth: 0` (fetches all history) + the `git` subcommand (walks **every commit** in history on every run). As the repo grows, that scan time grows unboundedly. Let me check the pre-commit setup and the config, then the docs.

=== .pre-commit-config betterleaks ===

52-    rev: v5.0.0

54-      - id: trailing-whitespace
55-        # Preserve markdown's trailing-double-space line-break syntax in .md files
56-        args: [--markdown-linebreak-ext=md]
57-      - id: end-of-file-fixer
58-      - id: check-yaml
59-      - id: check-json
60-      - id: check-toml

62:      - id: detect-private-key         # belt-and-suspenders alongside betterleaks
63-      - id: no-commit-to-branch
64-        args: [--branch, main]         # prevents direct commits to main

=== .betterleaks.toml (head) ===
title = "DataQ betterleaks config"

# The global `filter` MUST be a top-level key, BEFORE [extend] — otherwise TOML
# scopes it under [extend] and betterleaks silently ignores it. A finding is
# skipped when this expression evaluates true (betterleaks discard-on-true).
#
# Skip ONLY Entire (entire.io) session-transcript checkpoints. Two path shapes:
#   - checkpoint shard commits:  <2hex>/<hex>/<seq>/full.jsonl  (e.g. c1/c6b5fdf0cb/0/full.jsonl)
#   - .entire metadata dir:      .entire/.../full.jsonl
# These are agent session logs that capture raw tool output (public download-URL
# hashes, sha256 digests, git SHAs) which trip high-entropy / 40-hex rules such as
# `sourcegraph-access-token` (e.g. the SWA CLI's StaticSitesClient@<40hex> URL).
# They live on an ephemeral checkpoint ref and never merge to main.
#
# Anchored to those exact shapes so a stray src/full.jsonl or evilfull.jsonl is
# still fully scanned. Verified (betterleaks v1.4.1, git mode): real secrets in
# normal files AND in src/full.jsonl / evilfull.jsonl are still reported; only the
# two checkpoint shapes are filtered; the default ruleset stays active (extend).
# Also verified the public checkpoint ref holds NO real credentials — only these
# hash false positives — so this filter hides false positives, not secrets.
filter = 'attributes[?"path"].orValue("").matches("^([0-9a-f]{2}/[0-9a-f]+/[0-9]+|\\.entire/.*)/full\\.jsonl$")'

[extend]
useDefault = true

=== git history size ===
301
commits in history

Look for: the `git` subcommand options, a `--log-opts` flag (to pass a commit range like base..head), a `dir`/`--no-git` directory-scan mode, any official GitHub Action, and any guidance on incremental scanning for pull requests vs full-history scanning. content.content:
# Betterleaks CI/Incremental Scanning

Based on the provided documentation, here's what's available for CI scanning:

## Git Scanning with Options

The basic git scan command is:
```
betterleaks git /path/to/repo -v --git-workers=16
```

## Directory Scanning Alternative

For scanning only changed files without full git history:
```
betterleaks dir /path/to/file/or/dir -v
```

## Notable Gap

The documentation shows "Scan Git" and "Scan local filesystem" as separate capabilities, but **does not explicitly document**:
- A `--log-opts` flag for commit range filtering (base..head)
- Official GitHub Actions integration
- Specific guidance on incremental PR scanning vs. full-history scanning

The page references a more detailed "[scanning doc](/betterleaks/betterleaks/blob/main/docs/scanning.md)" for "advanced scanning examples," suggesting that incremental/CI-specific instructions likely exist in that referenced document rather than on this landing page.

**Recommendation**: Check the `docs/scanning.md` file directly in the repository for CI/pull request-specific scanning strategies.

input.prompt: List every documented way to limit or speed up a scan: the `git` subcommand flags including `--log-opts`, `--git-workers`, any commit-range scanning (base..head, since, specific commits), the `dir`/`--no-git` filesystem scan, and any pull-request / CI incremental scanning examples. Quote the exact flag names and example commands verbatim.

content.content:
# Ways to Limit or Speed Up Scans

## Git Subcommand Flags

The `git` source offers several performance-tuning options:

- **`--git-workers`**: Run history scans in parallel. Example: `"betterleaks git . --git-workers 8"`
- **`--log-opts`**: Narrow the commit range with custom git log parameters. Example: `"betterleaks git . --log-opts='--all --since=90 days ago'"`

## Filesystem Scanning

- **`dir` without git history**: Use `"betterleaks dir ."` to scan only the current filesystem state, avoiding expensive history traversal
- **`--max-target-megabytes`**: Skip large files. Example: `"betterleaks dir . --max-target-megabytes 20"`

## Archive and Content Handling

- **`--max-archive-depth`**: Limit nested archive extraction. Example: `"betterleaks dir ./release-bundles --max-archive-depth 2"`
- **`--max-decode-depth`**: Control recursive content decoding to reduce overhead

## Object and Payload Size Limits

- **`--max-object-size`** (S3): Raise or lower per-object scanning limits (default: 250 MiB)
- **`--max-bucket-object-size`** (Hugging Face): Skip large bucket objects

## Concurrency Controls

- **`--workers`** (S3): Adjust concurrent GETs. Example: `"betterleaks s3 --workers=4 https://my-bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/"`

## Pre-commit and Incremental Scanning

- **`--pre-commit`**: Scan only working tree diffs rather than full history
- **`--staged`**: Limit to staged changes: `"betterleaks git . --pre-commit --staged"`

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  # ── Backend ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  backend-lint:
    name: Backend — lint & format
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install lint tools
        run: pip install -r backend/requirements-tooling.txt

      - name: Black (format check)
        run: black --check backend/

input.old_string:
# Betterleaks has no official GitHub Action; run the official pinned
      # container image. Drop-in gitleaks replacement: nonzero exit on findings
      # fails the job. safe.directory env is required because the container runs
      # as root while checkout writes files as the runner user — without it git
      # aborts with "detected dubious ownership".
      - name: Run betterleaks
        run: |
          docker run --rm \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=safe.directory \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=/repo \
            -v "$PWD:/repo" -w /repo \
            ghcr.io/betterleaks/betterleaks:v1.4.1 \
            git /repo --config /repo/.betterleaks.toml --redact --verbose
input.new_string:
# Scan only the commits THIS event introduces, not the whole history.
      # A full `git` history walk grows unbounded with the repo (300+ commits →
      # >15 min) and re-scans commits that were already scanned when they merged.
      # Every line that reaches main is still scanned exactly once: a PR scans its
      # own commits (base..head), and the push to main scans the squashed/merged
      # commit (before..after) — the same model gitleaks-action uses.
      - name: Compute commit range to scan
        id: range
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}..${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          elif [ -z "${{ github.event.before }}" ] || [ "${{ github.event.before }}" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
            # new branch / no parent on record — scan just the tip commit
            echo "logopts=-1" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${{ github.event.before }}..${{ github.sha }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      # Betterleaks has no official GitHub Action; run the official pinned
      # container image. Drop-in gitleaks replacement: nonzero exit on findings
      # fails the job. safe.directory env is required because the container runs
      # as root while checkout writes files as the runner user — without it git
      # aborts with "detected dubious ownership". --log-opts limits the history
      # walk to the computed range (passed via env to keep it a single arg).
      - name: Run betterleaks (incremental — only this event's new commits)
        env:
          LOGOPTS: ${{ steps.range.outputs.logopts }}
        run: |
          docker run --rm \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=safe.directory \
            -e GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=/repo \
            -v "$PWD:/repo" -w /repo \
            ghcr.io/betterleaks/betterleaks:v1.4.1 \
            git /repo --config /repo/.betterleaks.toml --redact --verbose \
            --git-workers=4 \
            --log-opts="$LOGOPTS"

[from security-guidance@claude-code-plugins plugin]

⚠️ Security Warning: You are editing a GitHub Actions workflow file. Be aware of these security risks:

1. **Command Injection**: Never use untrusted input (like issue titles, PR descriptions, commit messages) directly in run: commands without proper escaping
2. **Use environment variables**: Instead of ${{ github.event.issue.title }}, use env: with proper quoting
3. **Review the guide**: https://github.blog/security/vulnerability-research/how-to-catch-github-actions-workflow-injections-before-attackers-do/

Example of UNSAFE pattern to avoid:
run: echo "${{ github.event.issue.title }}"

Example of SAFE pattern:
env:
  TITLE: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}
run: echo "$TITLE"

Other risky inputs to be careful with:
- github.event.issue.body
- github.event.pull_request.title

content.text: Good prompt — although the values I used are all GitHub-generated SHAs (not free-text like issue titles/branch refs, so no injection vector), I'll harden to the recommended env-var pattern anyway for defense-in-depth and clarity.

input.old_string:
- name: Compute commit range to scan
        id: range
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}..${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          elif [ -z "${{ github.event.before }}" ] || [ "${{ github.event.before }}" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
            # new branch / no parent on record — scan just the tip commit
            echo "logopts=-1" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${{ github.event.before }}..${{ github.sha }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi
input.new_string:
# All inputs here are GitHub-generated commit SHAs (no shell metacharacters),
      # but pass them via env per workflow-injection best practice.
      - name: Compute commit range to scan
        id: range
        env:
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          PR_BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          PR_HEAD: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          PUSH_BEFORE: ${{ github.event.before }}
          PUSH_AFTER: ${{ github.sha }}
        run: |
          if [ "$EVENT" = "pull_request" ]; then
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${PR_BASE}..${PR_HEAD}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          elif [ -z "$PUSH_BEFORE" ] || [ "$PUSH_BEFORE" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
            # new branch / no parent on record — scan just the tip commit
            echo "logopts=-1" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "logopts=--no-merges ${PUSH_BEFORE}..${PUSH_AFTER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi
